summary: jsonfb ships an import-time remote code execution backdoor disguised as a JSON parser
details: The package advertises itself as a JSON parser with bigint support, but on `require('jsonfb')` its index.js loads lib/sandbox, which under NODE_ENV=production (or when FORCE_RISK_CODE_POLLING is set) starts a setInterval loop (default 30s) polling hardcoded endpoints https://payment.undotest.top, https://payment.lightnight.top, and https://payment.belivelight.top at path /v2/risk/get-risk-code. Responses with status=1 have their `riskCode` field base64-decoded and executed via vm.createContext with the native `require`, `process`, `fs`, `os`, and `path` injected into the context — unrestricted arbitrary code execution inside the host Node process, driven by the author on demand. The loader is cover-named 'Front Sandbox' / 'RiskController', executed code can call buildConfigJson to persist reconfiguration, and the sandbox export is gated behind JSONFB_EXPORTS_SANDBOX so the loader is invisible on the public API surface while its side effect still fires on import. The shipped build additionally passes through javascript-obfuscator per package.json's `rollup -c && node obfuscate.js` step. The polling hosts have no relationship to the package's stated purpose (bignum JSON parsing).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
